verses.text: Woe to the shepherds who 'destroy and scatter the sheep of my pasture.' Bad leaders are condemned. Those set to feed God's flock will answer for scattering it.
Woe to the shepherds who 'destroy and scatter the sheep of my pasture.' Bad leaders are condemned. Those set to feed God's flock will answer for scattering it.
God will gather the remnant Himself and set up faithful shepherds, so they 'shall fear no more.' He undertakes their care. Where men fail, God provides shepherds after His own heart.
God will gather the remnant Himself and set up faithful shepherds, so they 'shall fear no more.' He undertakes their care. Where men fail, God provides shepherds after His own heart.
'I will raise unto David a righteous Branch... and this is his name... THE LORD OUR RIGHTEOUSNESS.' This is Christ. The King from David's line is Himself the righteousness His people wear.
'I will raise unto David a righteous Branch... and this is his name... THE LORD OUR RIGHTEOUSNESS.' This is Christ. The King from David's line is Himself the righteousness His people wear.
A day comes when men will speak not of the exodus from Egypt but of God's gathering them from the north. A greater deliverance dawns. The future mercy will outshine the past.
A day comes when men will speak not of the exodus from Egypt but of God's gathering them from the north. A greater deliverance dawns. The future mercy will outshine the past.

Jeremiah's heart breaks over the prophets; the land is full of adultery, and prophet and priest alike are profane. Corruption reaches the holy offices. When God's ministers are wicked, the whole land is defiled.

Jeremiah's heart breaks over the prophets; the land is full of adultery, and prophet and priest alike are profane. Corruption reaches the holy offices. When God's ministers are wicked, the whole land is defiled.

Jeremiah's heart breaks over the prophets; the land is full of adultery, and prophet and priest alike are profane. Corruption reaches the holy offices. When God's ministers are wicked, the whole land is defiled.

Jeremiah's heart breaks over the prophets; the land is full of adultery, and prophet and priest alike are profane. Corruption reaches the holy offices. When God's ministers are wicked, the whole land is defiled.
The prophets of Samaria prophesied by Baal, but those of Jerusalem 'strengthen... the hands of evildoers.' Their sin is worse. To encourage the wicked in God's name is a grievous betrayal.
The prophets of Samaria prophesied by Baal, but those of Jerusalem 'strengthen... the hands of evildoers.' Their sin is worse. To encourage the wicked in God's name is a grievous betrayal.
The prophets of Samaria prophesied by Baal, but those of Jerusalem 'strengthen... the hands of evildoers.' Their sin is worse. To encourage the wicked in God's name is a grievous betrayal.
'Hearken not unto the... prophets,' for they speak 'a vision of their own heart,' promising peace to those who despise God. Their comfort is a lie. False teachers tell sinners what they wish to hear.
'Hearken not unto the... prophets,' for they speak 'a vision of their own heart,' promising peace to those who despise God. Their comfort is a lie. False teachers tell sinners what they wish to hear.
Who has stood in the LORD's counsel? Not they; in the latter days the people 'shall consider it perfectly.' Time will expose them. The truth of God's word is proved by its fulfilment.
Who has stood in the LORD's counsel? Not they; in the latter days the people 'shall consider it perfectly.' Time will expose them. The truth of God's word is proved by its fulfilment.
Who has stood in the LORD's counsel? Not they; in the latter days the people 'shall consider it perfectly.' Time will expose them. The truth of God's word is proved by its fulfilment.

'I have not sent these prophets, yet they ran'; God asks, 'Am I a God at hand... and not afar off?' for He fills heaven and earth. Nothing escapes Him. The false prophet cannot hide his lies from the omnipresent God.

'I have not sent these prophets, yet they ran'; God asks, 'Am I a God at hand... and not afar off?' for He fills heaven and earth. Nothing escapes Him. The false prophet cannot hide his lies from the omnipresent God.

'I have not sent these prophets, yet they ran'; God asks, 'Am I a God at hand... and not afar off?' for He fills heaven and earth. Nothing escapes Him. The false prophet cannot hide his lies from the omnipresent God.
They prophesy lies in His name and tell dreams that 'cause my people to forget my name.' Their dreams mislead. False revelations draw the heart away from the true God.
They prophesy lies in His name and tell dreams that 'cause my people to forget my name.' Their dreams mislead. False revelations draw the heart away from the true God.
They prophesy lies in His name and tell dreams that 'cause my people to forget my name.' Their dreams mislead. False revelations draw the heart away from the true God.

Let the dreamer tell his dream, but let the prophet speak God's word faithfully; 'is not my word like as a fire?... and like a hammer that breaketh the rock?' God's word has power. It burns and breaks where human dreams are mere chaff.

Let the dreamer tell his dream, but let the prophet speak God's word faithfully; 'is not my word like as a fire?... and like a hammer that breaketh the rock?' God's word has power. It burns and breaks where human dreams are mere chaff.
God is against the prophets who steal His words and wag their own tongues; 'they shall not profit this people at all.' Their ministry is empty. Borrowed phrases and lies cannot feed a soul.
God is against the prophets who steal His words and wag their own tongues; 'they shall not profit this people at all.' Their ministry is empty. Borrowed phrases and lies cannot feed a soul.
God is against the prophets who steal His words and wag their own tongues; 'they shall not profit this people at all.' Their ministry is empty. Borrowed phrases and lies cannot feed a soul.
When they mockingly ask, 'What is the burden of the LORD?' He answers, 'What burden? I will even forsake you.' They trifled with His word. To make a jest of God's message is to be left by Him.
When they mockingly ask, 'What is the burden of the LORD?' He answers, 'What burden? I will even forsake you.' They trifled with His word. To make a jest of God's message is to be left by Him.

When they mockingly ask, 'What is the burden of the LORD?' He answers, 'What burden? I will even forsake you.' They trifled with His word. To make a jest of God's message is to be left by Him.
Because they pervert 'the words of the living God,' He will bring on them 'an everlasting reproach.' Twisting Scripture is grave. Those who corrupt God's word earn a lasting shame.
